raw.title: Chinese Education Ministry proposes AI integration into school curricula, teaching materials
raw.text:
Chinese Education Ministry proposes AI integration into school curricula, teaching materials - Global Times
[CHINA](https://www.globaltimes.cn/china/index.html)/[SOCIETY](https://www.globaltimes.cn/china/society/index.html)
Chinese Education Ministry proposes AI integration into school curricula, teaching materials
By
[![](https://www.globaltimes.cn/Portals/0/attachment/2021/2021-02-03/66f7828e-54ff-4675-ba86-93a4912b9cab.png)](https://www.globaltimes.cn//author/Reporter-Liu-Caiyu.html)
[Liu Caiyu](https://www.globaltimes.cn//author/Reporter-Liu-Caiyu.html)
Global Times reporter on society, artificial intelligence and diplomatic relations.
[Liu Caiyu](https://www.globaltimes.cn//author/Reporter-Liu-Caiyu.html)
Published: Apr 16, 2025 10:32 PM
![Illustration: Liu Xiangya/Global Times](https://www.globaltimes.cn/Portals/0/attachment/2023/2023-10-12/9fb47026-9a40-48b2-ac8d-0442a6181b30.jpeg)
Illustration: Liu Xiangya/Global Times
China's Ministry of Education has released an opinion proposing to promote artificial intelligence (AI) to accelerate the country's education reform by integrating AI into the whole process of education, including textbooks and school curricula, media reported on Wednesday.
The opinion on accelerating the digitalization of education, issued by the Ministry of Education and eight other departments, proposes to deepen the application of large models in education, promote the intelligent upgrading of curricula, textbooks and teaching systems, integrate AI into the whole process of education and teaching, according to China Central Television on Wednesday.
The opinion is very important for teachers and students in primary, secondary, and higher education, as school curricula, textbooks, and teaching methods are all transitioning toward "digitalization" and "intelligent" upgrades, the report said.
At an event organized by the China Public Diplomacy Association on Wednesday, Lionel M. Ni, the founding president of the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou), highlighted the transformative impact of AI on education and called for adaptive strategies to harness its potential.
![Lionel M. Ni, the founding president of the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou), speaks at an event in Beijing organized by the China Public Diplomacy Association on April 16, 2025. Photo: Liu Caiyu/GT](https://www.globaltimes.cn/Portals/0/attachment/2025/2025-04-16/1e9b136e-4f6d-4e7b-ac3c-52a9d0b7b88c.jpeg)
Lionel M. Ni, the founding president of the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou), speaks at an event in Beijing organized by the China Public Diplomacy Association on April 16, 2025. Photo: Liu Caiyu/GT
He underscored AI's role in accelerating interdisciplinary integration, fostering collaboration among computer science, medicine, humanities, and other fields. This, he said, is giving rise to new academic disciplines and making possible the cultivation of "T-shaped talents," individuals with deep expertise and broad interdisciplinary knowledge, which is a central goal for higher education.
The opinion also said there will be integrated development of AI education across primary, secondary, and higher education, with universities establishing "general + specialized" AI general education courses and a number of smart courses, while primary and secondary schools offer relevant information technology courses and encourage the establishment of specialized AI courses.
For teachers, Ni said that they should now adapt to embrace AI. While AI is prevalent today, the teaching profession remains essential. However, Ni emphasized that new forms of teaching can be explored.
The released opinion also encourages the establishment of cloud schools, intelligent manufacturing spaces, and future learning centers, as well as the creation of a national-level experimental teaching center for "AI + X."
To develop new forms of teaching organization to promote changes in learning methods, it is important to build a "one-stop" digital student community, according to the opinion.
New models of human-machine collaborative teaching can also be integrated into the country's education system, the opinion read, so as to achieve large-scale personalized education driven by AI, and improve the efficiency and quality of education and teaching.
The renowned success of DeepSeek cannot continue if there is a lack of talents, Ni said, adding that "in the era of AI-driven revolution, the competition in algorithms is essentially a competition for talents."
For future talents, Ni told the Global Times they should "Be A-HERO in this transformative age of AI." The acronym "A-HERO" represents five essential qualities for future learners: A stands for AI literacy, H for High-Order Thinking, E for Ethical-thinking, R for Resilience, and O for Openness.
"China is exploring the use of AI to accelerate the country's education reform, aiming to serve as a model and promote its solutions for integrating

target.objectLabel: Chinese Education Ministry proposes AI integration into school curricula, teaching materials

raw.title: I.G. Jiang-Hu
raw.text:
I.G. JIANGXI AND HUGUANG
I.G.1. Archival Documents, Published
a. MQSL. Ser. 甲, vols. 2–4; ser. 丙, vols. 2, 6–10; ser. 丁, vols. 2, 8; ser. 己, 
vols. 1, 4, 6.
b. MQCZ. III: Hong Chengchou shiliao
c. MQDA. Ser. A, vols. 3–13, 17–19, 23–26, 28–37.
洪承疇史料, items 55, 57–60.
d. QNMD. Vols. 2–3 (see I.B.1.d.)
e. QNZS. Bk. 1, vols. 1–2.
f. “Qingdai dang’an shiliao” 清代檔案史料. In QDSC, 6 (1980): 129–361. This 
collection of 121 documents concerning Qing efforts to eradicate or bring to 
submission the late-Ming roving-rebel armies, and remnants thereof, includes 
much information on those rebels who at one time or another supported or professed allegiance to the YL regime—Liu Tichun 劉體純, Hao Yaoqi 郝搖旗, 
Li Laiheng 李來亨, Li Dingguo 李定國 (ECCP: 489–90), Sun Kewang 孫可
望 (ECCP: 679), to name the most important. (Names of rebel leaders often are 
written in documents using nicknames or homophonous characters, since the 
writers seldom had ready means of determining their actual names.) Some pertain to the capture of non-rebel followers of the YL court, and one item is the KX 
emperor’s announcement of the capture of the YL emperor in Burma. Documents 
of this sort range in date from SZ2 through KX3 and concern affairs not only in 
Huguang but also in Liang-Guang (see I.H.1.e), Sichuan (cf. I.I.3), and Yun-Gui.
For a detailed study of the repeated devastations suffered in northern Huguang during 
the Ming-Qing transition, based mainly on the sorts of published documents listed 
above, see Su Yunfeng 1988.
I.G.2. Bansheng ziji 半生自記, 2 j.
Wu Jinxi 吳晉錫: Fushe, 2/28b–29b.
Comp. Wu Yanlan 吳燕蘭 (author’s late-Qing clan descendant).
In MS collection Nangshu nang 囊書囊, fasc. 2. Copied for publication in a projected “Nanshe congshu” 南社叢書. Compiler’s fanli 1900. Prefaces 1901, 1920. 
Held Shanghai Library (ZSB.叢, #304).
In 1641 Wu Jinxi was appointed Judge for Yongzhou 永州 Pref. in far southern Huguang. This work records his efforts to effect some semblance of order and 
security in Yongzhou and neighboring Chenzhou 郴州 Pref. from that time until the 
autumn of 1647, when Qing forces under the “three princes”—Kong Youde 孔有德, 
Geng Zhongming 耿仲明, and Shang Kexi 尚可喜 (see II.32)—first penetrated 
I.G. Jiangxi & Huguang 285
southern Huguang. Wu initially faced local uprisings against one of the Ming imperial princes whose estate was in the region, then the invasions by the rebel armies 
of Zhang Xianzhong 張獻忠, then the mutiny of Ming Defense Commander Zuo 
Liangyu 左良玉, then the welter of armed units (under quasi-renegade Ming generals and erstwhile rebel leaders) that ransacked Huguang after the fall of Nanjing 
and the breakup of Li Zicheng’s 李自成 rebel regime, then the Manchus.
Wu did his best to serve the Ming Viceroy for Huguang, He Tengjiao 何騰蛟
(ECCP: 290–91) and He’s chief subordinate, Troop Supervising Intendant Zhang 
Kuang 章曠, though he may have gained peak effectiveness in civil-military collaboration with a former roving-rebel general, Hao Yaoqi (Hao Yongzhong 永忠). 
His account offers valuable information on the confusing Huguang theater and on the 
most important figures who were active there until 1647, as well as the perspective 
of an official in west-central China while the Ming capital migrated alarmingly from 
Beijing to Nanjing to Fuzhou to Guangzhou to elsewhere in the southwest.
Ref: Xie Guozhen 1981: 948.
I.G.3. Fang zhinan lu 倣指南錄 [Qianshi shimo 虔事始末], 1 j.
Fan Kangsheng 范康生: Anfu xianzhi 安福縣志 (1872): 11/22b; Yang Fengbao 
1960: 461.
Editions:
(a) In Jingtuo yishi, 37th wk. Rpt. in MQ, pt. 3, vol. 6. Also rpt. in BJ. 4, vol. 7.
(b) In ZNW, pt. 14.
(c) In TW, no. 239. 1967.
The author, a juren 舉人 of 1639, was a talented, high-minded writer who 
associated with Donglin 東林 and Fushe 復社 figures. A native of Ji’an 吉安
Pref., Jiangxi, he was quick to support Wan Yuanji (see I.C.17) at Wan’an 萬安, 
where the latter initiated anti-Qing resistance in southern Jiangxi after the fall of the 
HG court. Subsequently, Fan also aided Wan and Yang Tinglin 楊廷麟, appointed 
Viceroy and Minister of War, respectively, by the LW court in Fujian, in their determined stand against the Qing in Ganzhou 贛州 Pref. (honored with the name 
Zhongcheng 忠誠 Pref. by the LW emperor).
In this work, Fan recounts from prison his frustrated efforts to make contact with 
and coordinate actions among the disparate armed forces that were available in 
nearby regions to help defend strategically crucial Ganzhou—the gateway southward 
to Guangdong, and the node between southern Fujian and southern Huguang. Fan’s 
personal narration begins with an abortive resistance action at Zaokou 皂口 in Ji’an 
Pref., in the 3rd mo. of 1646, and continues through his capture when Ganzhou city, 
under direct siege for a month, fell bloodily to Qing forces on 10/4 of that year. The 
account is a study in how Ming leadership problems and consequent military disunity 
could be exacerbated by the self-martyring spirit of scholar-officials like Wan Yuanji.
Ref: Xie Guozhen 1981: 674–7

decision.rationale: 来源内容是关于历史档案的学术资料，标题和内容均未提及黄铁军，与目标人物无关，属于错误归因。

raw.title: Tencent launches Hunyuan foundation AI model for enterprises
raw.text:
Tencent unveils Hunyuan foundation AI model for enterprises as public debut of internet giant’s chatbot remains on hold | South China Morning Post
Advertisement
[Artificial intelligence](https://www.scmp.com/topics/artificial-intelligence?module=breadcrumb&pgtype=article)
[Tech](https://www.scmp.com/tech?module=breadcrumb&pgtype=article)[Big Tech](https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech?module=breadcrumb&pgtype=article)
# Tencent unveils Hunyuan foundation AI model for enterprises as public debut of internet giant’s chatbot remains on hold
### * Hunyuan is now available for enterprises in China to test and build apps via Tencent Cloud
* Tencent’s AI model has a range of functions, including image creation, copywriting and text recognition, that can be applied in multiple industries
Reading Time:**3 minutes**
[
Why you can trust SCMP
](https://www.scmp.com/policies-and-standards)
![Tencent Holdings introduced its foundation artificial intelligence model called Hunyuan at the company’s Global Digital Ecosystem Summit in Shenzhen on September 7, 2023. Photo: Handout](https://cdn.i-scmp.com/sites/default/files/styles/1020x680/public/d8/images/canvas/2023/09/07/d243d77f-66e1-439d-bee4-19f19a503d0d_c0403b72.jpg?itok=YJvthRVX&v=1694083977)
[Ann Cao](https://www.scmp.com/author/ann-cao)inShanghai
Published:6:53pm, 7 Sep 2023Updated:7:27pm, 7 Sep 2023
Chinese video gaming and social media giant[Tencent Holdings](https://www.scmp.com/topics/tencent?module=inline&pgtype=article)has launched its foundation[artificial intelligence](https://www.scmp.com/topics/artificial-intelligence?module=inline&pgtype=article)(AI) model called[Hunyuan](https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech/article/3233631/tencent-launch-ai-foundation-model-chatgpt-applications-amid-chinese-big-tech-jostling?module=inline&pgtype=article), as the country’s most valuable company bets on its vast ecosystem to help drive the adoption of more[ChatGPT](https://www.scmp.com/topics/chatgpt-and-generative-ai?module=inline&pgtype=article)-like services across the mainland.
Hunyuan, a large language model (LLM) that has more than 100 billion parameters and been pre-trained with over 2 trillion tokens, is now available for enterprises in China to test and build[apps](https://www.scmp.com/topics/apps?module=inline&pgtype=article)via the company’s cloud-computing arm[Tencent Cloud](https://www.scmp.com/presented/business/topics/china-markets-cloud-choice/article/3153173/unique-c2b-capability-china?module=inline&pgtype=article), according to a Thursday announcement by Tencent vice-president Jiang Jie at the main forum of the company’s 2023 Global Digital Ecosystem Summit in[Shenzhen](https://www.scmp.com/topics/shenzhen?module=inline&pgtype=article).
A foundation model is a deep-learning algorithm, trained on mountains of raw data from the[internet](https://www.scmp.com/topics/internet?module=inline&pgtype=article), that can be adapted to accomplish tasks in various AI applications. An early example of a foundation AI model is the LLM[GPT-3](https://www.scmp.com/tech/tech-trends/article/3209673/chatgpt-moon-landing-moment-ai-chinese-experts-say-tech-firms-try-dampen-enthusiasm?module=inline&pgtype=article)used by ChatGPT creator[OpenAI](https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech/article/3202427/openais-latest-chatbot-sending-chinese-users-frenzy-even-though-it-officially-unavailable-country?module=inline&pgtype=article).
Advertisement
Tencent’s Hunyuan provides a range of functions, including image creation, copywriting and text recognition, that can be applied in multiple industries such as[finance](https://www.scmp.com/topics/banking-finance?module=inline&pgtype=article),[social media](https://www.scmp.com/topics/social-media?module=inline&pgtype=article),[e-commerce](https://www.scmp.com/topics/ecommerce?module=inline&pgtype=article)and video gaming.
![A roadside view of Tencent Holdings’ headquarters in Shenzhen on August 9, 2023. The internet giant has already integrated its foundation artificial intelligence model, Hunyuan, into various business units. Photo: Bloomberg](https://img.i-scmp.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=contain,width=1024,format=auto/sites/default/files/d8/images/canvas/2023/09/07/fb0dc5a8-d666-4bc7-acd6-84bbfcbb81dc_34f54c2a.jpg "A roadside view of Tencent Holdings’ headquarters in Shenzhen on August 9, 2023. The internet giant has already integrated its foundation artificial intelligence model, Hunyuan, into various business units. Photo: Bloomberg")
A roadside view of Tencent Holdings’ headquarters in Shenzhen on August 9, 2023. The internet giant has already integrated its foundation artificial intelligence model, Hunyuan, into various business units. Photo: Bloomberg
This AI model is already integrated into the company’s various business units including Tencent Cloud, Tencent Games and[WeChat](https://www.scmp.com/topics/wechat?module=inline&pgtype=article). It is also being used in the company

raw.title: Yellow Sand Society
raw.text:
[Jump to content](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#bodyContent)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

19th and 20th century secret society and religious sect in China

| Yellow Sand Society |
| --- |
| [Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters): 黃沙會; [Wade–Giles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles): _Huang Sha Hui_ |
| [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/Single_Color_Flag_-_F8E854.svg/500px-Single_Color_Flag_-_F8E854.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Single_Color_Flag_-_F8E854.svg) The Yellow Sands are known to have used a yellow flag inscribed with their leaders' names during the insurgency in [Anyang County](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anyang_County) 1919–22.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry1980159-1) |
| Dates of operation | 19th–20th century |
| Active regions | Northern China ( [Henan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henan), Shandong, [Hebei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hebei)) |
| [Ideology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_political_ideologies) | - Militant [self-defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Self-defense) and [tax resistance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tax_resistance) - Heterodox [folk religion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_folk_religion) - [Ming](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ming_dynasty) [millenarian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Millenarianism) [monarchism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monarchism) |
| Allies | [Red Spear Society](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_Spear_Society) Heavenly Gate Society |
| Opponents | [Qing dynasty](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qing_dynasty) [Republic of China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Republic_of_China_(1912%E2%80%931949)) [Empire of Japan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Empire_of_Japan) [People's Republic of China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China) |
| Battles and wars | - [Xinhai Revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xinhai_Revolution) - [Warlord Era](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Warlord_Era) - [Second Sino-Japanese War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_Sino-Japanese_War) |
| **Preceded by** [White Lotus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_Lotus_Societies)(possibly) |

The **Yellow Sand Society**[\[a\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-name-4) ( [Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters): 黃沙會; [Wade–Giles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles): _Huang Sha Hui_),[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-FOOTNOTETai1985141-5) also known as **Yellow Way Society** ( [Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters): 黃道會; [Wade–Giles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles): _Huang Tao Hui_),[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry1980270-6) and **Yellow Gate Society** ( [Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters): 黃門會; [Wade–Giles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles): _Huang Men Hui_),[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-FOOTNOTESlawinski197575-7) was a [rural secret society](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hui_(secret_society)) and [folk religious sect](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_salvationist_religions) in northern China during the 19th and 20th century.

Inspired by [millenarian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Millenarianism) ideas, the movement launched several uprisings against the late [Qing Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qing_dynasty), the [Republic of China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Republic_of_China_(1912%E2%80%931949)), and the Chinese [puppet states](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puppet_state) of [Japan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Empire_of_Japan). The Yellow Sands were eventually suppressed by the [Chinese Communist Party](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party) (CCP) in the second half of the 20th century.

## History

\[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Yellow_Sand_Society&action=edit&section=1)\]

### Origins

\[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Yellow_Sand_Society&action=edit&section=2)\]

Similar to other [secret societies in China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hui_(secret_society)), the exact origins and operations of the Yellow Sand Society are difficult to discern. Chinese secret societies occasionally changed names, and were highly decentralized, with several differently named branches operating as part of the same movement. Sometimes, completely unrelated groups also shared the same name. It is thus difficult to clearly differentiate between the secret societies.[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-FOOTNOTESlawinski1972210-8)[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow_Sand_Society#cite_note-FOOTNOTESlawinski197575-7) The Yellow Sands are known to have operated as "Yellow Way Society" for part

decision.rationale: 来源页面为维基百科“Yellow Sand Society”词条，未提及“黄铁军”，属于高置信度人名错配，应安全删除。

- **摘要:**
安维峻是晚清时期一位忠君爱国、勤政爱民、廉洁奉公、不惧权贵、不畏强权、敢于直言劝谏的英雄式人物，他与清流群体成员不仅有着较为密切的私人交际联系，还在思想观念以及具体作为等方面保持着高度的相似性和一致性。甲午中日战争爆发前后，安维峻积极谏言，纠正和惩处了一些地方官员的不当行为，对地方实力派也起到了一定的警示和震慑作用；但是安维峻维护皇权，打击地方实力派的主观目的性太过强烈，有些奏疏内容显得牵强附会，闻风奏事的固有缺陷与耿介直言的谏言风格最终为自己招致了祸端。纵观安维峻的政治生涯，他有着清流的良知、血性和气节，向世人展现了“铁汉”儒者的形象。与此同时，安维峻也与其他大多数清流人士一样，有着思想狭隘、保守甚至迂腐的一面，他对外界新变化知之甚少，晚年言行逐渐逆时代潮流，盲目忠君、一味复古、反对维新变革，渐失昔日刚劲之气。安维峻是晚清清流群体的典型代表人物，他与许多清流人士在社会经历、贡献、局限性等方面存在大量相似之处，人们对安维峻的固化印象也体现了对清流群体的笼统认知。通过解析安维峻及其清流生涯，可以管窥晚清政局下的清流政治。



**关键词:** - [安维峻](javascript:void(0);) /
- [铁汉](javascript:void(0);) /
- [清流](javascript:void(0);) /
- [晚清](javascript:void(0);) /
- [《谏垣存稿》](javascript:void(0);) /
- [甲午战争](javascript:void(0);)

**Abstract:**
An WeiJun was a heroic figure in late Qing Dynasty, who was diligent in work, care about the people, brave to speak out and admonish. He not only has a relatively close personal communication with the number of Ching-liu (“清流”) group, but also maintains a high degree of similarity and consistency in terms of ideology and specific actions. Around the outbreak of the Sino-Japanese War in 1894-1895, An Weijun advised actively, corrected and punished some local officials for their improper behaviors, which also played a certain warning and deterrence role to the local forces. But An Weijun maintained the imperial power, the subjective purpose of cracking down on local forces is too strong, some memorial to the throne is far-fetched, the inherent defects of listening to rumors and outspoken style of advice eventually led to disaster for himself. Throughout the political career of An Weijun, he has a clear conscience and consciousness, but also has a clear blood and integrity, he showed the world the “iron man” Confucian scholar image. At the same time, An Weijun, like most other Ching-liu people, has narrow-minded, conservative and even pedantic side. He knew little about the new changes in the outside world. After the outbreak of the Sino-Japanese War in 1894-1895, he went against the trend of the times for a long time, gradually losing his vigor of the past. An Weijun was deeply influenced by Neo-Confucianism, adhered to and pursued the ideological line of Ching-liu for most of his life. He became famous for being honest and admonition. However, in his later years, he eventually became a victims in the old age of the late Qing Dynasty. An Weijun was a typical representative of the late Ching-liu group, He had many connections with Ching-liu people in terms of social experience, contributions, shortcomings and deficiencies. By understanding the stereotyping of An Weijun’s personal image and the process of stereotyping, we can briefly recognize of the Ching-liu politics under the political situation of the late Qing Dynasty.

**Key words:** - [An WeiJun](javascript:void(0);) /
- [Iron man](javascript:void(0);) /
- [Ching-liu](javascript:void(0);) /
- [late Qing Dynasty](javascript:void(0);) /
- [_The Draft of Remonstrance_](javascrip

| 姓名： | [兰哲同](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E5%2585%25B0%25E5%2593%25B2%25E5%2590%258C&sfield=author) |

| 提交日期： | 2024-06-05 |
| 奖项名称： | [北京高校优秀本科毕业设计（论文）](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E5%258C%2597%25E4%25BA%25AC%25E9%25AB%2598%25E6%25A0%25A1%25E4%25BC%2598%25E7%25A7%2580%25E6%259C%25AC%25E7%25A7%2591%25E6%25AF%2595%25E4%25B8%259A%25E8%25AE%25BE%25E8%25AE%25A1%25EF%25BC%2588%25E8%25AE%25BA%25E6%2596%2587%25EF%25BC%2589&sfield=awardname) |
| 颁奖单位： | 北京市教育委员会 |
| 获奖时间： | [2024](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=2024&sfield=winningtime) |
| 外文题名： | Furnace and Iron Flow: The formation of mass masses and local Red Army in the edge of Hunan and Jiangxi (1927-1929) |
| 中文关键词： | [湘赣边缘地带](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E6%25B9%2598%25E8%25B5%25A3%25E8%25BE%25B9%25E7%25BC%2598%25E5%259C%25B0%25E5%25B8%25A6&sfield=all) ; [革命根据地](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E9%259D%25A9%25E5%2591%25BD%25E6%25A0%25B9%25E6%258D%25AE%25E5%259C%25B0&sfield=all) ; [群众武装](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E7%25BE%25A4%25E4%25BC%2597%25E6%25AD%25A6%25E8%25A3%2585&sfield=all) ; [地方红军](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E5%259C%25B0%25E6%2596%25B9%25E7%25BA%25A2%25E5%2586%259B&sfield=all) ; [暴动](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E6%259A%25B4%25E5%258A%25A8&sfield=all) ; [内生型割据](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=%25E5%2586%2585%25E7%2594%259F%25E5%259E%258B%25E5%2589%25B2%25E6%258D%25AE&sfield=all) |
| 外文关键词： | [The edge of Hunan and Jiangxi](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=The%2Bedge%2Bof%2BHunan%2Band%2BJiangxi&sfield=all) ; [Revolutionary base](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=Revolutionary%2Bbase&sfield=all) ; [Mass armed forces](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=Mass%2Barmed%2Bforces&sfield=all) ; [Local Red Army](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=Local%2BRed%2BArmy&sfield=all) ; [Rebellion](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=Rebellion%2B&sfield=all); [Endogenous separation.](https://bklib.ruc.edu.cn/simpsearch.action?keyword=Endogenous%2Bseparation.&sfield=all) |
| 中文摘要： | ︿ 近代史学界在湘赣边缘地带群众武装与地方红军生成的专门研究方面存在学术空白。湘赣边缘地带的“群众武装”“地方红军”与“主力红军”三个概念是动态变化的，“脱产性”与“地方性”是界定三者的重要标准。江西省农民自卫军是湘赣边缘地带各革命根据地群众武装的前身，《江西省农民自卫军组织大纲》将群众武装分为江西省民自卫军警备队和农民自卫军义勇队，建构了一个理想化的群众武装模式。永新暴动产生的赣西农民自卫军是湘赣边缘地带各革命根据地群众武装的雏形。永新暴动具有原生性与偶然性，促进了井冈山革命根据地赤卫队公开守土传统的形成。万安暴动诞生了中国共产党独立领导下的群众武装，并且培育了地方红军的雏形，进行了有效的“内生型割据”，探索出一个乡村苏维埃革命的党、政、军体系。东固暴动建立了一套区别于万安的独特武装生成路径与秘密割据方式。东固等地武装整编形成的江西工农革命军第三师第七纵队是该地区第一支中国共产党领导下的正规军队。东固暴动为中国共产党探索了一条新的“红军”生成路径。革命武装的生成力量具有多样化与动态性，“内生型割据”与“外力型割据”及其衍生出来的军事序列和武装生成路径观点不应该被过多地套用。在红四军的指导下，东固的秘密割据与游击战经验被推广给湘赣边界，在当地形成了一种“公开”与“秘密”结合的新革命模式。中国共产党群众武装与地方红军生成的对立面是国民党对地方反共武装的培育、统合。这一“地方军事化”过程中，国民党治下的地方精英农村统治体系形成了。湘赣地区的吉安、赣州两大城市，都不具备生成工人武装的主客观因素。 ﹀ |
| 外文摘要： | ︿ At present, there is a lack of papers on the generation of the mass armed forces and local Red Army in the The edge of Hunan and Jiangxi . The three concepts of "mass armed forces ", "local Red Army" and "main Red Army" in the The edge of Hunan and Jiangxi are dynamically changing." Full-time soldiers "and" Local " are important criteria to define the three concepts. Jiangxi peasant Self-defense Army is the predecessor of the Communist Party of Chinas mass armed forces in the The edge of Hunan and Jiangxi area. The Outline of Jiangxi Peasant Self-defense Army divides the mass armed forces into the Jiangxi Civil self-defense force and the peasant self-defense volunteer team, constructing an idealized mass armed mode. The peasant self-defense army in western Jiangxi produced by the Yongxin Rebellion was the prototype of the mass armed forces in the The edge of Hunan and Jiangxi . The origin and contingency of the Yongxin Rebellion promoted the formation of the tradition of the red guards in the Jinggangshan revolutionary base. The Wan'an Rebellion gave birth to the mass armed forces under the independent leadership of the Communist Party of China, and cultivated the prototype of the local Red Army.Wan'an Rebellions made an effective "Endogenous separation", and explored a party, political

raw.title: 黄仁勋专访：从洗碗工，到 AI 教父
raw.text:
黄仁勋专访：从洗碗工，到 AI 教父- 爱氧气[![爱氧气](https://www.iyangqi.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/20240429082137281538.svg)](https://www.iyangqi.com/)
**
**
[登录](https://www.iyangqi.com/login?modal-type=login)[注册](https://www.iyangqi.com/register?modal-type=register)
[原创投稿](https://www.iyangqi.com/tougao)
****
1. [爱氧气首页](https://www.iyangqi.com)
2. **[前沿科技](https://www.iyangqi.com/tech)
# 黄仁勋专访：从洗碗工，到AI 教父[赛博禅心](https://www.iyangqi.com/member/30)•2024年3月23日 上午11:30•[前沿科技](https://www.iyangqi.com/tech)
英伟达的老黄在斯坦福接受了一个采访，信息量很大，包括公司治理和人工智能发展，及「如黑夹克缺货，你穿什么」的尖锐问题，我给翻译了下，并逐行改了3遍。
![黄仁勋专访：从洗碗工，到 AI 教父](https://www.iyangqi.com/wp-content/themes/justnews/themer/assets/images/lazy.png)
**主持人：**
老黄，太荣幸了！谢谢你能来🎉Jensen, this is such an honor. Thank you for being here.
**老黄：**
很高兴能来这儿，谢谢～I’m delighted to be here. Thank you.
**主持人：**
为了纪念您重返斯坦福，我想我们可以从您初次离开时刻开始讨论。那时您加入了LSI Logic，这家公司当时特别火！
您在科技界建立了非凡的声誉，并与一些最著名的科技公司合作。然而，最终您还是决定离开，去创立自己的公司。为什么呢？In honor of your return to Stanford, I decided we’d start talking about the time when you first left, you joined LSI Logic, and that was one of the most exciting companies at the time. You’re building a phenomenal reputation with some of the biggest names in tech, and yet you decide to leave to become a founder. What motivated you?
**老黄：**
因为克里斯和柯蒂斯。当我还是一名工程师时，克里斯和柯蒂斯在Sun 工作。我有幸与包括安迪·贝克托斯海姆在内的一些计算机科学界最杰出的人物一起工作，共同开发了工作站、图形工作站等等。Chris and Curtis, Chris and Curtis, I was an engineer atlogic and Chris and Curtis were at Sun and I was working with, with, some of the brightest minds in computer science at the time of all time, including Andy bektosheim and others building, building work stations and graphics workstations and so on and so forth.
**老黄：**
有一天，克里斯和柯蒂斯说他们想离开Sun，并希望我能想出他们下一步该做什么。
虽然我当时已有一份很棒的工作，但他们坚持要与我一起创立一家公司。因此，每当他们来访时，我们就会在Danny’s 餐厅聚会。顺便提一句，那是我母校，也是我创立的第一家公司，在成为CEO 之前，我的第一份工作是洗碗工。And Chris and Curtis, said one day that they like to leave Sun and they like me to go figure out what they’re going to go be for and, I had a great job, but they insisted that I figure out, you know, with them how to, how to build a company. And so, so we hung out of Danny’s whenever they drop by and, which was, which is, by the way, my Alma martyr, my first company, you know, my first job before for before CEO was a, was a dishwasher.
**老黄：**
其实我做得一直还都不错。总之，我们聚在一起，那是在微处理器革命期间，大约在1992 年到1993 年间，PC 革命刚刚开始。那时候，革命性的Windows 95 显然还未上市，奔腾处理器甚至还没宣布。所以，这一切都是在PC 革命之前。显然，微处理器将会非常重要。我们想，为什么不创立一家公司，来解决普通计算机无法解决的问题呢？这就成了公司的使命——制造一种类型的计算机，解决普通计算机无法解决的问题。直到今天，我们依然专注于此。如果你看看我们因此而开拓的市场，你会发现，像计算药物设计、天气模拟、材料设计等问题，都是我们非常自豪的成就。And so, and I did that very well and so anyways, we got together and we just, and then was during the microprocessor revolution, this is 19 ninetythree and 19 ninetytwo when we’re getting together, the PC revolution was just getting going. You know, that Windows 95 obviously, which is the revolutionary version of Windows, didn’t even come to the market yet, and Pentium wasn’t even announced yet. And so it’s, and this is, this is all before the right before the PC revolution. And it was, it was pretty clear that that, the microprocessor was going to be very important. And we thought, you know, why don’t we build a company to go to solve problems that a normal computer that is powered by general purpose computing can’t. And so that, that became the company’s mission, to go to go build a computer, the type of computers and solve problems that normal computers can. And to this day, we’re focused on that. And if you look at all the problems that, that in the markets that we opened up as a result, it’s, you know, things like computational drug design, weather simulation, materials design, these are all things that we’re really, really proud of.
**老黄：**
机器人技术、自动驾驶汽车、我们所说的人工智能软件。我们推动了技术的发展，使得计算成本几乎降至零，这开启了一种全新的软件开发方式，即由计算机自己编写软件。Robotics, self driving cars, autonomous, autonomous, software we call artificial intelligence. And then all, you know, of course, we, we drove the, the technology so hard, that, that eventually the computational cost, went to approximately 0 and then enabled enable the whole new way of developing software where the computer wrote the software itself.
**老黄：**
你没听错，就是人工智能，从那时候开始，我们上路了。Artificial intelligence is, we know it. And so, so I, that, that was, that was it, that was the journey.
**主持人：**
对！Yeah.
**老黄：**
后面的大家也都看到了😊thank you all for coming.
**主持人：**
是的，这些东西现在充满了我们的生活。让我们回头看，在当时，LSI Logic的CEO说服了他最大的投资者Don Valentine与您见面。显然，他是Sequoia的创始人。
很多的创始人都在预测和创造未来，在这个过程中，您是如何说服硅谷VC 大佬们去押注一个初创团队，真金白银地支持一个面向未来的产品呢？Well, these applications are on all of our minds today. We’re back. Then the CEO of LSI Logic convinced his biggest investor Don Valentine to meet with you. He is obviously the founder of Sequoia. Now I can see a lot of founders here edging forward in anticipation, but how did you convince the most sought after investor in Silicon Valley to invest in a team of first time founders building a new product for a market that doesn’t even exist?
**老黄：**
在最一开始，我不知道如何写商业计划，所以我去了一家书店，那时还有书店😊😊😊在商业

decision.rationale: 该来源为黄仁勋的专访，与黄铁军无关，属于人名错配的高置信删除候选。
